heading: Cyber Crimes Center, Child Exploitation Investigations Unit, Computer Forensics Unit, and Cyber Crimes Unit
label: 473 Cyber Crimes Center, Child Exploitation Investigations Unit, Computer Forensics Unit, and Cyber Crimes Unit

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–22, title III, § 302(a)129 Stat. 251May 29, 2015“Congress finds the following:“(1)The illegal market for the production and distribution of child abuse imagery is a growing threat to children in the United States. International demand for this material creates a powerful incentive for the rape, abuse, and torture of children within the United States.“(2)The targeting of United States children by international criminal networks is a threat to the homeland security of the United States. This threat must be fought with trained personnel and highly specialized counter-child-exploitation strategies and technologies.“(3)The United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement of the Department of Homeland Security serves a critical national security role in protecting the United States from the growing international threat of child exploitation and human trafficking.“(4)The Cyber Crimes Center of the United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement is a vital national resource in the effort to combat international child exploitation, providing advanced expertise and assistance in investigations, computer forensics, and victim identification.“(5)The returning military heroes of the United States possess unique and valuable skills that can assist law enforcement in combating global sexual and child exploitation, and the Department of Homeland Security should use this national resource to the maximum extent possible.“(6)Through the Human Exploitation Rescue Operative (HERO) Child Rescue Corps program, the returning military heroes of the United States are trained and hired to investigate crimes of child exploitation in order to target predators and rescue children from sexual abuse and slavery.”

Findings, , , provided that:Pub. L. 114–22, title III, § 302(a)129 Stat. 251May 29, 2015“Congress finds the following:“(1)The illegal market for the production and distribution of child abuse imagery is a growing threat to children in the United States. International demand for this material creates a powerful incentive for the rape, abuse, and torture of children within the United States.“(2)The targeting of United States children by international criminal networks is a threat to the homeland security of the United States. This threat must be fought with trained personnel and highly specialized counter-child-exploitation strategies and technologies.“(3)The United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement of the Department of Homeland Security serves a critical national security role in protecting the United States from the growing international threat of child exploitation and human trafficking.“(4)The Cyber Crimes Center of the United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement is a vital national resource in the effort to combat international child exploitation, providing advanced expertise and assistance in investigations, computer forensics, and victim identification.“(5)The returning military heroes of the United States possess unique and valuable skills that can assist law enforcement in combating global sexual and child exploitation, and the Department of Homeland Security should use this national resource to the maximum extent possible.“(6)Through the Human Exploitation Rescue Operative (HERO) Child Rescue Corps program, the returning military heroes of the United States are trained and hired to investigate crimes of child exploitation in order to target predators and rescue children from sexual abuse and slavery.”

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall operate, within United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement, Homeland Security Investigations, a Cyber Crimes Center (referred to in this section as the “Center”).

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The Center shall provide investigative assistance, training, and equipment to support domestic and international investigations of cyber-related crimes by the Department.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall operate, within the Center, a Child Exploitation Investigations Unit (referred to in this subsection as the “CEIU”).

children.label: i
children.text: child exploitation;

children.label: ii
children.text: child pornography;

children.label: iii
children.text: child victim identification;

children.label: iv
children.text: traveling child sex offenders; and

children.label: v
children.text: forced child labor, including the sexual exploitation of minors;

children.label: i
children.text: child exploitation prevention;

children.label: ii
children.text: investigative capacity building;

children.label: iii
children.text: enforcement operations; and

children.label: iv
children.text: training for Federal, State, local, tribal, and foreign law enforcement agency personnel, upon request;

children.label: C
children.text: shall provide training, technical expertise, support, or coordination of child exploitation investigations, as needed, to cooperating law enforcement agencies and personnel, which shall include participating in training for Homeland Security Investigations personnel conducted by Internet Crimes Against Children Task Forces;

children.label: D
children.text: shall provide psychological support and counseling services for United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement personnel engaged in child exploitation prevention initiatives, including making available other existing services to assist employees who are exposed to child exploitation material during investigations;

children.label: E
children.text: is authorized to collaborate with the Department of Defense and the National Association to Protect Children for the purpose of the recruiting, training, equipping and hiring of wounded, ill, and injured veterans and transitioning service members, through the Human Exploitation Rescue Operative (HERO) Child Rescue Corps program ; and1So in original. Probably should be “Program”.1

children.label: F
children.text: shall collaborate with other governmental, nongovernmental, and nonprofit entities approved by the Secretary for the sponsorship of, and participation in, outreach and training activities.

children.label: A
children.text: the total number of suspects identified by United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement;

children.label: i
children.text: the number of child victims identified through investigations carried out by United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of suspects arrested who were in positions of trust or authority over children;

children.label: C
children.text: the number of child exploitation cases opened for investigation by United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement; and

children.label: D
children.text: the number of child exploitation cases resulting in a Federal, State, foreign, or military prosecution.

children.heading: Availability of data to Congress
children.label: 4 Availability of data to Congress
children.text: In addition to submitting the reports required under paragraph (7), the CEIU shall make the data collected and maintained under paragraph (3) available to the committees of Congress described in paragraph (7).

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 5 Cooperative agreements
children.text: The CEIU is authorized to enter into cooperative agreements to accomplish the functions set forth in paragraphs (2) and (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to accept monies and in-kind donations from the Virtual Global Taskforce, national laboratories, Federal agencies, not-for-profit organizations, and educational institutions to create and expand public awareness campaigns in support of the functions of the CEIU.

children.heading: Exemption from Federal Acquisition Regulation
children.label: B Exemption from Federal Acquisition Regulation
children.text: Gifts authorized under subparagraph (A) shall not be subject to the Federal Acquisition Regulation for competition when the services provided by the entities referred to in such subparagraph are donated or of minimal cost to the Department.

children.label: i
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs of the Senate;

children.label: ii
children.text: the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate;

children.label: iii
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the Senate;

children.label: iv
children.text: the Committee on Homeland Security of the House of Representatives;

children.label: v
children.text: the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the Committee on Appropriations of the House of Representatives; and

children.label: B
children.text: make a copy of each report submitted under subparagraph (A) publicly available on the website of the Department.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall operate, within the Center, a Computer Forensics Unit (referred to in this subsection as the “CFU”).

children.label: i
children.text: United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement personnel; and

children.label: ii
children.text: Federal, State, local, tribal, military, and foreign law enforcement agency personnel engaged in the investigation of crimes within their respective jurisdictions, upon request and subject to the availability of funds;

children.label: B
children.text: shall provide computer hardware, software, and forensic licenses for all computer forensics personnel within United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement;

children.label: C
children.text: shall participate in research and development in the area of digital forensics and emerging technologies, in coordination with appropriate components of the Department; and

children.label: D
children.text: is authorized to collaborate with the Department of Defense, the National Association to Protect Children, and other governmental entities for the purpose of recruiting, training, equipping, and hiring wounded, ill, and injured veterans and transitioning service members, through the Human Exploitation Rescue Operative (HERO) Child Rescue Corps program .1

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 3 Cooperative agreements
children.text: The CFU is authorized to enter into cooperative agreements to accomplish the functions set forth in paragraph (2).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary is authorized to accept monies and in-kind donations from the Virtual Global Task Force, national laboratories, Federal agencies, not-for-profit organizations, and educational institutions to create and expand public awareness campaigns in support of the functions of the CFU.

children.heading: Exemption from Federal Acquisition Regulation
children.label: B Exemption from Federal Acquisition Regulation
children.text: Gifts authorized under subparagraph (A) shall not be subject to the Federal Acquisition Regulation for competition when the services provided by the entities referred to in such subparagraph are donated or of minimal cost to the Department.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall operate, within the Center, a Cyber Crimes Unit (referred to in this subsection as the “CCU”).

children.label: A
children.text: shall oversee the cyber security strategy and cyber-related operations and programs for United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement;

children.label: i
children.text: cyber economic crime;

children.label: ii
children.text: digital theft of intellectual property;

children.label: iii
children.text: illicit e-commerce (including hidden marketplaces);

children.label: iv
children.text: Internet-facilitated proliferation of arms and strategic technology; and

children.label: v
children.text: cyber-enabled smuggling and money laundering;

children.label: i
children.text: United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement personnel; and

children.label: ii
children.text: Federal, State, local, tribal, military, and foreign law enforcement agency personnel engaged in the investigation of crimes within their respective jurisdictions, upon request and subject to the availability of funds;

children.label: D
children.text: shall participate in research and development in the area of cyber investigations, in coordination with appropriate components of the Department; and

children.label: E
children.text: is authorized to recruit participants of the Human Exploitation Rescue Operative (HERO) Child Rescue Corps program for investigative and forensic positions in support of the functions of the CCU.1

children.heading: Cooperative agreements
children.label: 3 Cooperative agreements
children.text: The CCU is authorized to enter into cooperative agreements to accomplish the functions set forth in paragraph (2).

children.heading: HERO Child-Rescue Corps2So in original. “Program” probably should be inserted at end of heading.2
children.label: e HERO Child-Rescue Corps2So in original. “Program” probably should be inserted at end of heading.2

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: There is established within the Center a Human Exploitation Rescue Operation Child-Rescue Corps Program (referred to in this section as the “HERO Child-Rescue Corps Program”), which shall be a Department-wide program, in collaboration with the Department of Defense and the National Association to Protect Children.3So in original. Probably should be “Operative”.3

children.heading: Private sector collaboration
children.label: B Private sector collaboration
children.text: As part of the HERO Child-Rescue Corps Program, the National Association to Protect Children shall provide logistical support for program participants.

children.heading: Purpose
children.label: 2 Purpose
children.text: The purpose of the HERO Child-Rescue Corps Program shall be to recruit, train, equip, and employ members of the Armed Forces on active duty and wounded, ill, and injured veterans to combat and prevent child exploitation, including in investigative, intelligence, analyst, inspection, and forensic positions or any other positions determined appropriate by the employing agency.

children.label: A
children.text: provide, recruit, train, and equip participants of the Program in the areas of digital forensics, investigation, analysis, intelligence, and victim identification, as determined by the Center and the needs of the Department; and

children.label: i
children.text: child exploitation;

children.label: ii
children.text: child pornography;

children.label: iii
children.text: unidentified child victims;

children.label: iv
children.text: human trafficking;

children.label: v
children.text: traveling child sex offenders; and

children.label: vi
children.text: forced child labor, including the sexual exploitation of minors.

children.heading: Paid internship and hiring program
children.label: f Paid internship and hiring program

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary shall establish a paid internship and hiring program for the purpose of placing participants of the HERO Child-Rescue Corps Program (in this subsection referred to as “participants”) into paid internship positions, for the subsequent appointment of the participants to permanent positions, as described in the guidelines promulgated under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Internship positions
children.label: 2 Internship positions
children.text: Under the paid internship and hiring program required to be established under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall assign or detail participants to positions within United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement or any other Federal agency in accordance with the guidelines promulgated under paragraph (3).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary shall promulgate guidelines for assigning or detailing participants to positions within United States Immigration and Customs Enforcement and other Federal agencies, which shall include requirements for internship duties and agreements regarding the subsequent appointment of the participants to permanent positions.

children.heading: Preference
children.label: B Preference
children.text: The Secretary shall give a preference to Homeland Security Investigations in assignments or details under the guidelines promulgated under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Term of internship
children.label: 4 Term of internship
children.text: An appointment to an internship position under this subsection shall be for a term not to exceed 12 months.

children.label: A
children.text: not less than the minimum rate of basic pay payable for a position at level GS–5 of the General Schedule; and

children.label: B
children.text: not more than the maximum rate of basic pay payable for a position at level GS–7 of the General Schedule.

children.heading: Eligibility
children.label: 6 Eligibility
children.text: In establishing the paid internship and hiring program required under paragraph (1), the Secretary shall ensure that the eligibility requirements for participation in the internship program are the same as the eligibility requirements for participation in the HERO Child-Rescue Corps Program.

children.heading: Hero Corps hiring
children.label: 7 Hero Corps hiring
children.text: The Secretary shall establish within Homeland Security Investigations positions, which shall be in addition to any positions in existence on , for the hiring and permanent employment of graduates of the paid internship program required to be established under paragraph (1).December 21, 2019

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to the Secretary such sums as are necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Allocation
children.label: 2 Allocation
children.text: Of the amount made available pursuant to paragraph (1) in each of fiscal years 2022 through 2027, not more than $10,000,000 shall be used to carry out subsection (e) and not less than $2,000,000 shall be used to carry out subsection (f).
